questions.text: What does Documentation mean in Core 2 Objective 4.0 Operational Procedures?

answers.text: Documentation records support information, technical procedures, assets, incidents, service expectations, and repeatable processes.
Support systems information management organizes ticket, device, asset, procedure, and knowledge information so support teams can work consistently.
Ticketing systems track support requests, incidents, progress, ownership, severity, escalation, and resolution.
Ticket user information identifies the affected user and provides contact, location, department, or role details needed for support.

explanations.a: Documentation records support information, technical procedures, assets, incidents, service expectations, and repeatable processes. This is the correct answer.
explanations.b: Support systems information management organizes ticket, device, asset, procedure, and knowledge information so support teams can work consistently. That describes Support systems information management, not Documentation. Documentation records support information, technical procedures, assets, incidents, service expectations, and repeatable processes. Pick this only if the question were about Support systems information management.
explanations.c: Ticketing systems track support requests, incidents, progress, ownership, severity, escalation, and resolution. Ticketing systems and Documentation are easy to confuse, but Documentation records support information, technical procedures, assets, incidents, service expectations, and repeatable processes. The correct concept here is Documentation.
explanations.d: Ticket user information identifies the affected user and provides contact, location, department, or role details needed for support. This statement is about Ticket user information, but the question targets Documentation. Documentation records support information, technical procedures, assets, incidents, service expectations, and repeatable processes. This option is therefore incorrect.

questions.text: A technician is following a procedure involving Documentation. Which answer best describes its purpose or behavior?

Documentation records support information, technical procedures, assets, incidents, service expectations, and repeatable processes. This option is therefore incorrect.
explanations.d: A configuration management database stores details and relationships for assets, services, configurations, and support dependencies. Configuration management database is a separate concept. Documentation records support information, technical procedures, assets, incidents, service expectations, and repeatable processes. So this choice misses the mark.

questions.text: What does Workstation backup and recovery mean in Core 2 Objective 4.0 Operational Procedures?

answers.text: Workstation backup and recovery protects user data and restores access after deletion, failure, corruption, ransomware, or replacement.
A synthetic full backup creates a full backup from previous backup data without rereading all source data from the client.
Recovery restores data, systems, or access from backup or other protected copies.
In-place overwrite recovery restores data back to its original location, replacing existing data there.

explanations.a: Workstation backup and recovery protects user data and restores access after deletion, failure, corruption, ransomware, or replacement. This is the correct answer.
explanations.b: A synthetic full backup creates a full backup from previous backup data without rereading all source data from the client. That defines Synthetic full backup, not Workstation backup and recovery. Workstation backup and recovery protects user data and restores access after deletion, failure, corruption, ransomware, or replacement. So this choice does not fit.
explanations.c: Recovery restores data, systems, or access from backup or other protected copies. That fits Recovery, not Workstation backup and recovery. Workstation backup and recovery protects user data and restores access after deletion, failure, corruption, ransomware, or replacement. Therefore this answer is wrong.
explanations.d: In-place overwrite recovery restores data back to its original location, replacing existing data there. That describes In-place overwrite recovery, not Workstation backup and recovery. Workstation backup and recovery protects user data and restores access after deletion, failure, corruption, ransomware, or replacement. Pick this only if the question were about In-place overwrite recovery.

questions.text: A technician is following a procedure involving Workstation backup and recovery. Which answer best describes its purpose or behavior?

answers.text: In this scenario, A backup is a copy of data, applications, system state, or configuration used for recovery.
In this scenario, Workstation backup and recovery protects user data and restores access after deletion, failure, corruption, ransomware, or replacement.
